title: Amphiasma divaricatum
taxonomy: Amphiasma divaricatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to Western Australia and is often found growing in heath or scrubland. The classification of Amphiasma divaricatum is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Asterids, Order - Ericales, Family - Ericaceae, Genus - Amphiasma, Species - A. divaricatum. Amphiasma divaricatum is a relatively rare plant, and as such, its classification is important for conservation efforts. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and relationships of this unique species.

intro: Amphiasma divaricatum, also known as Yellow Chamomile or Wild Chamomile, is a perennial plant in the daisy family with yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in relieving anxiety, insomnia, and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Amphiasma divaricatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Amphiasma divaricatum is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin issues.
